lines: - *   # The metrics are per-process and only exposed on mongod today.
- *   # TODO SERVER-123932: relax once updateLookup metrics are aggregated on mongos.
- *   # The lookup runs on the node serving the getMore; pin reads to that node so serverStatus reads
- *   # the same process that recorded the metric.
+ *   # The 'gone' document's post-image lookup relies on observing the delete that immediately
+ *   # follows its update. The lookup's read concern is {level: "majority", afterClusterTime:
+ *   # <the update's own clusterTime T1>}, a minimum bound, not "now". A secondary servicing
+ *   # that read may not yet have majority-committed (or advanced its per-batch lastApplied past)
+ *   # the later delete at T2 > T1, so it can legitimately still see the pre-delete state.
import {before, beforeEach, after, afterEach, describe, it} from "jstests/libs/mochalite.js";
@@ -74,7 +74,7 @@ describe("change stream updateLookup single-document-lookup metrics", function (
// The single-document-lookup engine that enriched an updateLookup change event with its post-image.
// The serverStatus key under 'changeStreams.updateLookup' is the value of each constant.
- * Computes numeric difference of server status metrics between 'after' and 'before'.
+ * combine() numeric value of server status metrics between 'a' and 'b'.
-        // Treat a missing before-leaf as 0 so lazily-created metrics (absent in before-snapshot)
-        // produce a correct delta rather than NaN.
// NumberLong / NumberInt override valueOf() to return a primitive JS number.
+            const aVal = a != null && typeof a.valueOf === "function" ? a.valueOf() : 0;
-        // BSON leaf types with no sensible before/after delta (point-in-time values, not counters).
-        const kNonDiffableTypes = [Timestamp, Date, ObjectId, BinData, NumberDecimal];
+        const kNonCombinableTypes = [Timestamp, Date, ObjectId, BinData, NumberDecimal];
+                result[key] = _combineMetrics(a != null ? a[key] : undefined, b[key], combine);
// Helper object for retrieving change stream metrics from the 'serverStatus' command's output.
-    // Snapshots the full serverStatus metrics before fn(), runs fn(), and returns the delta
-    // (after - before), treating a missing before-leaf as 0. Non-plain-object and non-numeric
-    // leaves hold the after value unchanged.
+     * Returns serverStatus metrics summed across every data-bearing node in the fixture (every
+     * node of a plain replica set, or every node of each shard for a sharded cluster).
+        // Sum the per-node metrics into a single cluster-wide snapshot before diffing.
+     * Snapshots getSsMetricsAcrossCluster() before fn(), runs fn(), and returns the delta (after -
+     * before), treating a missing before-leaf as 0. Callers don't need to know or care whether
+     * they're talking to a replica set or a sharded cluster.
+ * Tests the optimized change stream updateLookup runtime-disable kill-and-resume behavior: a
+ * change stream cursor whose updateLookup stage was wired on the optimized path while
+ * featureFlagChangeStreamOptimizedUpdateLookup was on must be throwing a resumable error
+ * at its next getMore once the flag is off. The stream then resumes from its token on the
+ * legacy aggregation path and keeps returning correct fullDocument values.
+ *   # Raw getMore assertions on a server-killed cursor's exact error code/label would be
+    it("retires the optimized cursor and resumes on the legacy path", () => {
+            // Open the optimized-path stream and confirm it.
+            // Ensure the optimized-path read doesn't run the legacy aggregation engine.
+                "the aggregation engine ran a lookup while the optimization was on",
+                // Ensure getMore command fails on the existing change stream cursor since IFR flag is turned off.
+                    msg: "optimized cursor was not retired with a resumable error after the flag was disabled",
+                // Reopen tha change stream, since the flag is off, change stream must be reopened on the
+                // Ensure the unoptimized-path read runs the legacy aggregation engine.
+                    "expected exactly one aggregation-engine updateLookup for the resumed stream",
+ * Polls getMore until it fails with a resumable change stream error.
+export function assertGetMoreFailsWithExpectedError({db, cursorId, collName, expectedError, msg}) {
+        const res = db.runCommand({getMore: cursorId, collection: collName});
// Keeps track of cursors opened during the test so that we can be sure to
// clean them up before the test completes.

it("fails with retryable error when shard primary steps down (readPref: primary)", function () {

it("resumes correctly after readPreference enforcement error", function () {
+ * For change stream cursors whose updateLookup stage was wired on the optimized (Express/SBE) path,
+ * enforces that the optimization feature flag is still enabled. If an operator has turned the flag
+ * off since the cursor was opened, kills the cursor and throws a resumable RetryChangeStream error,
+ * so the stream is reopened (built fresh with the flag's current value) on the legacy Aggregation
+ * This check is a no-op for cursors that never took the optimized path (non-change-stream cursors,
+ * cursors opened with the flag off, or streams that simply do not do an updateLookup).
+    // NOTE: Reading a fresh feature flag value, as opposed to snapshoted one, since we want to
+    // determine if the flag value has changed from the time the executor has been created.
+    // The flag got turned off after this cursor was wired on the optimized path. Kill the cursor
+    // before throwing and surface a resumable change stream error.
+              "Optimized change-stream updateLookup was disabled; resuming on the legacy path");
+ * The single method for all change-stream getMore preconditions.
+    // Check if readPreference can still be satisfied after possible elections.
+    // Check if the updateLookup optimization flag was turned off on the updateLookup cursor.
* Validates that the lsid of 'opCtx' matches that of 'cursor'. This must be called after
* authenticating, so that it is safe to report the lsid of 'cursor'.
-            // Check that readPreference can still be satisfied after possible elections.
+            // Run the change stream getMore preconditions before iterating the cursor.
// Get the read concern level here in case the cursor is exhausted while iterating.
+    // Record the effective wiring on this operation so it rides onto the ClientCursor. The getMore
+    // precondition uses it to kill-and-resume the cursor if the flag is later turned off.
+    // True iff this change stream cursor's updateLookup stage was wired on the optimized
+    // (Express/SBE) path. Recorded on the ClientCursor so the getMore precondition can
+    // kill-and-resume the cursor if the optimization flag is turned off.
// The hash of the PlanCache key for the query being run. This may change depending on what
@@ -196,6 +196,10 @@ class ClientCursor : public Decorable<ClientCursor>, public TransactionResources
@@ -540,6 +544,11 @@ class ClientCursor : public Decorable<ClientCursor>, public TransactionResources
// Flag if the current cursor is used for a change stream query.
+    // True iff this cursor's updateLookup stage was wired on the optimized (Express/SBE) path. Used

// Flag to decide if diagnostic information should be omitted.
+    // Pass along 'usesOptimizedUpdateLookup' so it detects IFR flag change at runtime.
